title: magnolia nitida var. lotungensis
intro: Magnolia nitida var. lotungensis, also known as lotung magnolia, is a species of evergreen tree native to southern China, characterized by its dark green leaves and showy white flowers. The bark of the lotung magnolia is used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The medicinal properties of lotung magnolia are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of lotung magnolia, such as tea or tincture, are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
